entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Dependent Management: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
1. Overview:
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central allows organizations to manage employees' dependents efficiently. This enables HR to store and maintain dependents' details for benefits administration, tax calculations, etc.
2. HRIS Elements:
According to SAP KBA 2807618, in SuccessFactors Employee Central, dependents are managed through the Dependents Information Portlet, which includes:

   • A specific HRIS element: personRelationshipInfo
   • Reused fields from various HRIS elements (as listed in the table)

Limitations:

  • Limited customization for reused fields
  • No RBP support for reused HRIS elements

 Note 1: You can add custom fields under the personRelationshipInfo HRIS element, and they can be managed via RBP, newly added fields will be unchecked by default in RBP, so you’ll need to manually enable permissions for them as needed

 Note 2: If you need to customize fields from reused HRIS elements, DO NOT modify the original element, as changes will affect both Dependents and Employees. Since RBP is not supported, create a new Person Type under the base HRIS element instead.

Navigation Path: Manage business configuration→Select the base HRIS element → configure New Person Type.

Select the Person Type as Dependent then save.

Now A new HRIS element has been created for Dependents, allowing customization such as disabling fields, changing visibility, and sorting. However, some limitations remain as it is still linked to the base PersonalInfo element.
Limitations:
   • Field labels cannot be changed.
   • In correction mode, fields display only their identifiers.                                                                                     • Custom fields appear as String20, Date1, etc. So  Always refer to the main HRIS element to correctly identify fields before making changes.

We can repeat the same steps for the PersonInfo if any customizations are needed.

Note 3: According to KBA 2634641 & KBA 3235376, National ID and Home Address fields for dependents CANNOT be managed using RBP, even by creating a new Person Type.
Available Options:
   ✓ Hide the entire section from the Dependents Information Portlet
   ✓ Keep it as it is (no RBP control possible)
To Hide the Address section from dependents portlet:

Select the main HRIS homeAdress→Make correction →Disabled under Dependents Section = YES

To Hide the National ID section from dependents portlet:

Select the main HRIS NationalID→Make correction →Disabled under Dependents Section = YES

3. User Interface and RBP:
Access to the Dependents UI (portlet) will be Based on:
   • Users' ability to view or modify certain fields depends on their permissions.
   • Customizations previously done will influence what is displayed.
To grant permission to the users:
Access Manage Permission Roles→ Select the desired role and click Edit→ Skip Editing the basic info by clicking the ‘NEXT’ button→ In the Add Permission screen, search for Dependents, grant the necessary permissions, and save.

Example: Visa File Number is a custom newly added field under the personRelationshipInfo

Adding Information about Dependents (ESS):
   • The UI will show relevant fields based on the employee permissions.
   • Users can Add, Edit, and delete details for dependents.
   • Make sure to click the “Edit details” button at the bottom of the dependents screen and fill in all required fields to avoid warnings, errors, or information messages.

4. Bulk Data Management:

4. a. Importing Dependents details:

According to SAP KBA 2092840, We have to download 3 main uploading templates from Import Employee Data:
1. Biographical Information: To create the dependent as a new person in the system:

Navigation Path: Access Import Employee Data→ Select Download Template →Select
Biographical Information→ Select the desired fields From Available data fields Tab→ Click
Generate Template.

Repeat the same steps to download the remaining templates.

Give the code for the country of birth(for example TUN for Tunisia).(A list of some country codes will be added to the end of this article)

Fill in the DOB: MM/DD/YYYY

user ID: keep blank

Person ID External: This will be the unique identifier for the dependent and will be reused for
other uploading templates.
Note: Make sure to enter a unique ID that has never been used before for any other
person type, as this is the same template used to upload employee data.

2. Person Relationships: To create the link between the employee and his dependents:

Person ID External: Fill with the employee ID
Related Person Id External: Give the Dependent Unique ID created in the previous template.    Relationship: Enter the value according to your configured relationship picklist (refer to the Picklist Center). Do the same for any value that will be added to a picklist-type field.

Note: Customized fields created under personRelationshipInfo will appear in this Template.

3. Personal Information: Upload the dependents' personal data such as First name, Last name…:

For Home Airport and Salutation: Enter the value according to your configured picklist (refer to
Picklist Center). Do the same for any value that will be added to a picklist-type field.

 

Note: You can add additional Information for the dependents like NationalID info…
Data Uploading: 

Access Import Employee Data→ Select Import Data→ FIRST Select Biographical Information→ Select the Data import  File→ Validate then Import

Repeat the same steps to Import the remaining templates In the same order 1. biographical info 2.Relationship 3.personalInfo.

4. b. Exporting Dependents data:

Please refer to SAP Public KBA: 2806252 - How to Retrieve Dependents Related Information via Canvas Reports. It provides a detailed and clear guide on exporting dependent details.

However, please note that the Dependent Related Person ID extracted in the report is different from the Dependent Unique ID created during the import. To pull the exact ID used during the import, add the Dependent Person ID column under the Dependent Personal Info table (Step 3 in the second screenshot)

4. C. DELETE/ DELIMIT Dependents: 

To DELETE an employee's dependent record from the Dependents portlet in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central can be challenging, especially when dealing with large amounts of data.
Performing a Full Purge import requires reimporting all dependent data, and if you want to completely delete records by uploading an empty file, the system will not allow it.


As per SAP KBA 2411208, the Person Relationship operation does not support a DELETE function but instead uses the DELIMIT option. Here are the two methods to handle the deletion of dependent records:
  ✓ If Data is Available in the Import Template:
   • In Incremental import mode, add the DELIMIT operation to the Person Relationship record in the import template.
   • Ensure that the Effective Start Date for the operation matches the Effective Start Date of the dependent record.
   • When the DELIMIT operation is executed, it will effectively "close" the relationship record for that dependent and Remove it from the History. But Dependents data will remain in the system.
   • Further steps: You can Add the DELETE operation to the Personal Info, and the remaining used Import files accordingly

  ✓ If Data is not Available in the Import Template:
   • Step 1: Retrieve Person ID External or Dependent Person ID via Data Inspector or canvas Report (exported in ‘Exporting Dependents data’ Section)
   • Step 2: Export and fill Person Relationship template with Person ID External employee ID, Related Person ID External, Dependent Person ID, event date, relationship-type and DELIMIT in Operator column.
   • Step 3: Import fie in incremental mode and verify that dependent is no more shown in employee's profile.


For Additional details, You can Follow the Guidelines mentioned in SAP Public KBA 2274679 - How To Delete/Delimit Dependents Data via Import - Employee Central.


To DELIMIT a dependent relationship while keeping it as historical data, you should add the DELIMIT operator in the import template, and ensure the Effective Start Date is different from the record's start date. This will mark the dependent record as inactive without deleting it from the system, allowing it to remain in the system as historical data.
Example:
If the dependent's original Effective Start Date is 2023-01-01, and you want to delimit the record as of 2024-01-01, you would:
• Add the DELIMIT operation in the import template.
• Set the Effective Start Date for the DELIMIT operation to 2024-01-01, which is the new date when the relationship will be ended.


This approach ensures that the dependent's record is still stored as historical data but is no longer active as of the effective start date of the DELIMIT operation.

 

Country  Alpha-3 Code
Tunisia TUN
India IND
Germany DEU
United States USA
France FRA
United Kingdom GBR
Canada CAN
Australia AUS
Italy ITA
Spain ESP
Netherlands NLD
Belgium BEL
Switzerland CHE
Austria AUT
Sweden SWE
Norway NOR
Denmark DNK
Finland FIN
Japan JPN
China CHN
India IND
Brazil BRA
South Africa ZAF
Russia RUS
Mexico MEX
Saudi Arabia SAU
United Arab Emirates ARE

entry.title: Secure connection configuration in Employee Central and Employee Central Payroll in Grow with SAP
entry.content:
As we know that EC(Employee Central) and ECP (Employee Central Payroll) are interdependent  and they communicate with each other .Recently I got opportunity to  configuration connection between EC to ECP system on SAP on public cloud This blog post  describes step-by-step in how to configure connection with help of OAuth 2.0  which is the industry standard protocol for authorization.

The prerequisites:

1-Check, in which support package your service is available.

2-Parameter icm/HTTPS/verify_client is set to 1 (accept certificates) or 2 (require certificates).

3-Make sure that you’ve maintained the optional fields for System ID and API .

4-Activate SAP Gateway

5-Ensure that the Local Provider follows the format <System ID>_<Client>,

6-Check system alias for OData service id not then create it

Configuration step

1-Certificate generation

1-Login on EC system Go to Admin Center  Security Center and choose X509 Certificates

 

 

 

Choose Add to create a New X509 Certificate.

 

 

Please  create 2 certificate as per below naming format

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ADM

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ESS

Select SAP Cloud Root CA as Certification Authority (CA

 

 

 

Click on Generate and Save, to save your certificate key and download

 

 

 

2-Configuring OAuth Identity Provider

Login on ECP system and run t-code SAML2

 

 

It will open saml2 screen in new window click on trusted provider then Add   Manually

You need to add 2 trusted provider format should be as below-

<EC Instance Name>_ADM,

<EC Instance Name>_ESS

Choose next and Primary Signing Certificate and upload certificate which you have created in SC system and save it  you must perform activity for both provider which is mention above

 

Once both providers created then go to main screen and change it to

In the details section of your newly created Identity Provider, choose Add. In the Supported NameID Formats window, select Unspecified and choose OK.

In the details section of NameID Format "Unspecified", select Logon ID  or login alias as User ID Mapping Mode.If you are using SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication for Employee Central Payroll, then select Logon Alias as User ID Mapping Mode.

 

 

 

 

3-Creating Service Users

 

 

Go to transaction SU01 in ECP system and create following service users:

EC_ADM_OAUTH

 

 

 

 

 

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 

 

 

4-Registering OAuth Client

login on ECP system and rum t-code SOAUTH2 in OAuth 2.0 Administration  choose Create  create below 2

EC_ADM_OAUTH

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 In the Create OAuth 2.0 Client window, select a OAuth 2.0 Client ID, provide a Description and choose Next.

In the Client Authentication step, ensure SSL Client Certificate is checked and choose Next.

In the Resource Owner Authentication step, ensure Grant Type SAML 2.0 Bearer Active is checked. In the Trusted OAuth 2.0 IdP field, choose the identity provider you already created in the Configuring OAuth Identity Provider section and choose Next.

In the Scope Assignment step, add a OAuth 2.0 Scope ID, according to your client ID and choose Next:

Final screen will be as below-

 

 

5-Importing X.509 Certificates

Run strust t-code in ECP system and import previously create certificates from EC system in SSL server standard

 

 

 

 

 

6-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table USREXTID

1 Run transaction SM30, open view VUSREXTID in edit mode.

2 Enter DN in the External ID type field.

3 Choose New Entries and import the certificate.

4 Provide following user names: EC_ESS_OAUTH

5 Set the Activated indicator to activate the client certificate logon for the user.

6 Save your entries and the assignment of External ID to Users will be displayed.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

7-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table CERTRULE

1-Go to transaction CERTRULE.

2-Switch to edit mode and import your certificate in the Subject field.

3-Choose the Explicit Mapping button.

4- Select user EC_ESS_OAUTH and continue.

5-The new entries are displayed in the Explicit Mappings tab.

6-Save your entries.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

Now you can check it at ECP system it will ask for EC system credential and authenticate from EC system

 

 

Reference-

ahttps://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_PAYROLL/185f14fbe60d4bbb8d7d5e4f8d89b24b/bb1f11be38134ae3aac6b3139297ffc5.html

entry.title: How to Enable and Benefits of Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024
entry.content:
Introduction

In the 2H 2024 release of SAP SuccessFactors, the Latest People Profile feature has been introduced to improve how employee data is presented and accessed within the system. This feature unifies all employee information into a comprehensive, modernized profile that is both easy to use and visually appealing. In this blog post, we will guide you step-by-step through the process of enabling and using the Latest People Profile.

Key Benefits of the Latest People Profile

Before we dive into the setup process, let’s first highlight the key benefits of the Latest People Profile:

  • Unified View: Access all key employee data in a single, intuitive profile.
  • Modern User Interface: The profile comes with a fresh, user-friendly layout.
  • Better Accessibility: Employees, HR, and managers can access information with minimal clicks.
  • Improved Decision Making: With all data in one place, decisions are easier to make with real-time insights.

 

How to Enable the Latest People Profile

Step 1: Log in to SAP SuccessFactors

  1. Begin by logging into your SAP SuccessFactors account with administrator credentials.

Step 2: Navigate to Admin Center

  1. Once logged in, go to the Admin Center. This is your central hub for all configuration tasks.
  2. In the Admin Center, find Company Settings.

Step 3: Open Company System and Logo Settings

  1. Under Company Settings, select Company System and Logo Settings.

 

Step 4: Enable Latest People Profile

  1. In the Company System and Logo Settings, locate the option Enable Full Profile for Latest People Profile.
  2. Check the box to enable the feature.
  3. Click Save to apply the changes.

How to Use the Latest People Profile

For HR Professionals

  1. Access Employee Profiles: HR professionals can now access a unified profile for each employee. This profile provides quick access to key employee data, from personal details to performance records.

  2. View Key Data: The profile consolidates essential information like job history, competencies, and compensation. You can even see learning progress, performance reviews, and more—all on one page.

For Managers

  1. Access Real-Time Data: Managers can use the Latest People Profile to view up-to-date information about their team, from performance reviews to development plans.

  2. Track Employee Progress: With an integrated profile, managers can track team members’ learning progress, performance reviews, and development goals.

For Employees

  1. Self-Service Capabilities: Employees can view and manage their personal information, including contact details, performance feedback, and career development records.

  2. Transparency in Career Progression: Employees can access their performance reviews, career goals, and other relevant information, making it easier for them to track their growth.

Step 5: Customize the Latest People Profile (Optional)

Once you’ve enabled the Latest People Profile, you can customize it according to your organization's needs:

  1. Modify Profile Layout: Adjust the layout of the profile sections to suit your needs (e.g., move sections like Performance Reviews to the top).

  2. Add Custom Fields: Include any custom fields that are specific to your business.

Conclusion

Enabling the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 provides your organization with a modern, streamlined, easy-to-navigate profile for employees. With a unified view, improved accessibility, and modernized user interface, it offers HR professionals, managers, and employees a more efficient way to access crucial information.

By following the simple steps outlined above, you can enable and start using this powerful feature today!

Additional Resources:

  • Link to SAP SuccessFactors Help page for more detailed guides.

entry.title: Useful way to control rules triggering when a candidate comes from Recruitment & Onboarding
entry.content:
If you are deploying Onboarding into an instance that is already live with Employee Central, then during the implementation will need to be mindful of the rules that have already been created.    If you have data created in recruitment and then mapped to Onboarding, then rules may disrupt the data by triggering again. 

For example, in Employee Central we have the following rule that opens recurring pay components and populates the data.  However, when data is sent from recruitment this data can be stripped out by the rule triggering again, especially for ‘oninit’ rules where you can’t control the context.

One alternative is to configure the solution below.

1. Create a picklist as follows.

2. Create a custom field on ‘employmentinfo’ as shown below and assign this to employee and onboardee person types.

3. In recruitment create a custom field with the picklist and using a rule set the value to RCM

4. Map this to the custom field to the field ‘employmentinfo’. 

5. Build the following rule and assign this ‘onchange’ to a field in job information.  This will set the value = ONB if you manually start onboarding.

6. Set the context as follows.

7. Create this rule.

8. Assign this rule on change of a field in job information with the following context.  This will set the value = EC when you manually add a hire in Employee Central.

This will now ensure the custom field in employment info is populated as follows:

  • Sent from Recruitment = RCM
  • Manually started in Onboarding = ONB
  • Hired directly into Employee Central = EC

You can now use the custom field to trigger rules to behave differently based on how you start the process.

Our original rule now looks like this:

This field is very useful in allowing other rules to behave different especially for ‘oninit’ rules when the rules context is not supported.

entry.title: DOMESTIC AND INTERNATIONAL CROSSBOARDING PROCESS DESIGN FOR A GLOBAL CLIENT
entry.content:
DOMESTIC AND INTERNATIONAL CROSSBOARDING PROCESS Design FOR A GLOBAL CLIENT

OVERVIEW:

We had a business requirement to allow crossboarding to trigger for both domestic and international transfers, when employee applied on a job through recruiting ONLY.

When an employee transitions to a new role within the same country, it was simple to design domestic crossboarding process with consistent configuration for job and employment information.

One of the challenges of international transfers was that each country has its own job and employment information field level configurations.

When a legal entity changes for international transfers, SAP advises terminating the record and starting a new hiring process; however, our client followed another process. Employees were transferred from one country to another, without terminating their existing records.

Initiating Legal Entity Transfer from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting | SAP Help Portal

Additionally, business did not provide clear event reason information that would initiate the crossboarding process for domestic and international transfers.

With this approach, we were also able to restrict the crossboarding trigger to only from recruiting ONLY, excluding changes from Employee Central. This aligns with one of the key requirements to limit the trigger point that we get to hear from many customers.

 

Explanation of business scenario:

Domestic Transfers – When a domestic employee moves to a different position/role within the same country, business wanted only minimum personal data review and validation procedures. From business perspective since the employees were local, it would be pointless to gather compliance data and additional onboarding paperwork particular to the country.     

International transfers – For international transfers when an employee was relocating abroad, business wanted employees to review/update their basic personal information, provide additional country-specific onboarding documentation, e-sign company-specific documents, and complete compliance paperwork. 

         

 

 

 

 With all the above scenario in place we did the below configuration to meet the business requirement.

 Configuration Steps:

  • Create two distinct event reasons specifically for the purpose of Domestic and International transfers cases

 

  • To prevent any kind of manual selection, created a business rule that will automatically select the correct event cause on job applications. This was a crucial step in preventing the initiation of an incorrect process variant an incorrect onboarding/crossboarding (Domestic or International) process.

 

  • Create different process variants, one for domestic and one for internation transfers cases

  Domestic Crossboarding Process

 International Crossboarding Process

 

 

 

  • With the help of business rule default, the correct process variant with the condition of event reason selected on job application

 

 

 

  • Created a Crossboarding trigger rule with the condition of event reason selected on job application

 

  • Set up position to job info synchronization business rule. This business rule is needed because of following below reason.

Data Consistency: This business rule ensures that the position data in SuccessFactors (SF) is consistent with the job information data. Crossboarding typically involves transferring an employee from one job or position to another. Without synchronization, discrepancies could occur, leading to mismatches in roles, responsibilities, or other vital position-related details.

 

Efficient Onboarding and Transitions: Crossboarding involves transitioning an employee from one position or location to another. By setting up synchronization between the position and job information, the system automatically updates the employee's job details, including job title, location, and department. This streamlines the process and ensures that everything is up to date for the new role without manual intervention.

 

Automation: The rule automates updates, reducing the need for manual input when a position or job changes. This minimizes human error, saves time, and ensures a smoother, more efficient transition between roles.

 

 

The remaining configuration is the same as for any other crossboarding process; we won't go into great depth because we have reference documents readily available for below steps.

  • Setting Up a Rule for Selecting the Onboarding Program
  • Permission Role for Future Manager
  • Business rule to Initiate Internal Hire Process.
  • Setting up Email Services
  • Recruit to Hire mapping.
  • Create multiple UI to get triggered to collect documents for international transfer cased for different countries via business rule.  

 

 Lesson Learned and Challenges faced:

  • To prevent the creation of position to job information sync business rules, SAP recently developed a few features (Enable Additional Internal Hire Activities). When this functionality was activated after SAP's release, we were able to initiate the crossboarding process without seeing any error messages in the BPE tile. Additionally, the job information portal was not updated with the new values we wanted to send from recruitment for new position/role. SAP's stated functionality did not assist in fulfilling our business needs. We tried using the feature as soon as it became available in their release.
  • We also noticed that I-9 compliance forms were not being triggered in international transfer cases. After much collaboration with SAP, they recommended a solution to manually trigger I-9 compliance for situations involving overseas transfers from non-US to US scenario. This issue was only with I-9 compliance forms; all other compliance forms for all countries were properly triggered for all international transfer cases.
  • Considering all the challenges we faced; SAP provides a standard process based on industry best practices and proven methodologies. Adhering to this process ensures that the Crossboarding workflow is implemented in a way that is both efficient and effective. By following the suggested process, companies avoid reinventing the wheel and benefit from SAP's expertise in designing optimized, scalable solutions.

entry.title: Enhancing Name Formatting in SuccessFactors with Business Rules
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Knowledge Article 2685847 highlights a common issue: the system’s inability to format multi-word names effectively. However, real-world scenarios often require first name as well as last name with proper capitalization (e.g., "Jean Paul"). This document presents a scalable solution leveraging business rules to overcome this limitation, ensuring names are formatted correctly with capitalized first letters for each word.

Problem Statement

  • Limitation: The existing solution only supports single-word capitalize first character of the name.
  • Impact: Multi-word names leading to data inconsistency and poor user experience.
  • Requirement: A mechanism to process multi-word names and capitalize the first letter of each word (e.g., "jean paul" → "Jean Paul").

Proposed Solution: Business Rules for Name Formatting

Using business rule, we can create a rule to process and format name fields dynamically.

Key Features

  • Handles single-word names (e.g., "jean" → "Jean").
  • Formats multi-word names (e.g., "jean paul" → "Jean Paul").
  • Ensures consistency across all name fields in the system.

Implementation Steps

Step 1: Define the Business Rule

Assign this rule to the first name field in HRIS element personalInfo.

Step 2: Testing and Validation

  • Test cases:
    • Single word: "jean" → "Jean"
    • Two words: "jean paul" → "Jean Paul"
    • Edge cases: "JEAN PAUL " → "Jean Paul", "jEaN pAUl" → "Jean Paul"

Input: Raw name string (e.g., "jean paul").

Output: Formatted name string (e.g., "Jean Paul").

Benefits

  • Flexibility: Supports two-word names.
  • Consistency: Enforces uniform capitalization across all name fields.
  • Scalability: Easily extensible to other text fields requiring similar formatting.

Conclusion

By leveraging business rules, users can bypass the single-word name formatting limitation outlined in Knowledge Article 2685847. This whitepaper provides a practical, reusable solution to format names with proper capitalization, enhancing data quality and user satisfaction. Future enhancements to use function replace() could include support for hyphenated names (e.g., "Jean-Pierre") or cultural naming conventions.

References

  • SAP Knowledge Article 2685847: Link

entry.title: Efficient Workflow Management: How to Streamline Stalled Workflow Reminder at Scale
entry.content:
Have you ever found yourself dealing with stalled workflows that hold up critical tasks? While it's easy to adjust a few workflows, what happens when you have hundreds of them? The thought of manually tracking and sending reminders for all those workflows can quickly become overwhelming. But it doesn't have to be that way.

In this article, we'll show you how to streamline the process and create an efficient solution that saves you time and effort.

Without wasting any time, let's dive straight into it. Before we implement the solution, there are few important things you should know. 

First, there are specific permissions required to proceed with the stepup. You'll need to have access to the Scheduled Job Manager. If you don't have this permission you can grant it, below is the process.

  1. Login to SAP SuccessFactors instance.
  2. Navigation to Admin Center -> Manage Permission Roles.
  3. Select the role for whom you want to grant access, Click on Edit.
  4. Under Administration Permission -> Admin Center Permissions. Check both the options Monitor Scheduled Jobs and Manage Scheduled Jobs.
  5. Click on Next and Save it.

Logout and logged in for testing.

To Send Workflow reminders efficiently, you can schedule a job using the Manage Scheduled Jobs feature. There are two ways to create a job:-

  • Provisioning
  • Admin Center

Since every consultant or admin doesn't have provisioning access, in this blog, we'll focus on how you can create a job directly from the Admin Center (Instance).

Here is the Step by Step Guide:- 

Step 1: Login to your SAP SuccessFactors Instance.

Step 2: Navigate to Admin Center -> Scheduled Job Manager.

Step 3: Click on Job Scheduler and after that on the right side, Click on + Create Job Request.

Step 4: Under Job Definition, Fill the required information as mentioned. 

  • Job Name: Fill as per your requirement. In this case, giving name as Workflow Reminder Notifications.
  • Job Type: Select as Workflow Action Reminder.
  • Job Owner: Who will receive all email related to job. Ideally it should be from Admin team or Super admin,
  • Job Parameters: Remind in Days, If you define any number here, System will send the reminder to approver in that interval time or if you wish the system should follow the configuration defined in Workflow, Leave it as blank.

In our requirement, we need to set it as 1.

  • Include Workflow Initiator in Workflow Reminders: As per your requirements you can select the checkbox.

In General, It's not required because it sends the reminder every time to initiator irrespective of their action on the workflow.

 

Step 5: UnderJob Occurence, Fill the information as per your requirement.

  • Occurence: Depends on your requirement, you can select.
    • One Time: The job will run only once.
    • Recurring: Job will run repeatedly, often at regular intervals defined by you.

Here our requirement is to send a daily reminder, we will select Recurring. 

  • Recurrence Pattern: Select any of the option available in the dropdown list.
    • Daily: It will run on daily basis.
    • Weekly:Job will run once in a week.
    • Monthly:Job will run once in a month.
    • Yearly: Job will run once in a year.

In our requirement, we need to run the job on daily basis. We are selecting Daily here.

  • Start: From when the Job will start.
  • End: Define the end date for job.

Step 6: Under Notification, Fill the required details.

  • Recipients: If you want to send a job update to any person except job owner, you can add thier mail id here.
  • Send Emails When Job Starts: If you want to send an email every time when the job starts, you can check this box. Ideally select this option only when the occurence is once. If you check this box and Occurence is recurrring, daily pattern, you will end with so many emails in your inbox.
  • Send Emails When Job Completes: Once the job is complete, System will send the notification over email.
  • Send Emails When Job Fails: This option is greyed out, as it is important if any job fails admin must be aware of that. 

Step 7: On the bottom right corner, Click on Save.

Step 8: Recheck all your configuration before submitting your job. You wouldn't be able to edit your job after submitting it.

Step 9: On Job Page, Against your job Click on Actions and Submit.

Step 10: If you want to run the job immediately. After Submitting the Job, click on Action and select Run It Now. 

Result:

After submitting the job, you can check the status under job monitor. If it's not visible under Job Monitor, you can check on Upcoming Jobs section.

entry.title: Ensuring Data Consistency in the Extraction of SuccessFactors Data to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
When transferring data from SuccessFactors to SAP Datasphere via the OData API, key challenges often arise around ensuring data completeness, accuracy, and integrity.

  1. Loss or omission of records during the transfer.
  2. Picklist attributes access.

 In this blog, we will dive into these challenges and offer a technical solution to ensure the data is accurately retrieved and mapped in the SAP Datasphere.

 

Ensuring Complete Data Transfers from SAP SuccessFactors to Datasphere via OData API

While extracting data from SF via OData API, users may encounter missing records in their target table. This issue typically arises due to pagination limitations in OData API or inconsistencies in the data extraction process due to large data volumes. Complete data transfer is essential for accurate reporting and analysis.

Scenario

  • Source: SuccessFactors OData API.
  • Destination: SAP Datasphere table.
  • Process: Data extraction via dataflows in SAP Datasphere.

Symptoms

  • Some records are not transferred to the target table.
  • Dataflow execution completes without errors.
  • Discrepancies are identified during data validation.

Root Cause

The OData API in SuccessFactors handles large datasets using pagination. Without proper configuration, the following issues can occur:

  • Dynamic Data Changes: Data in SuccessFactors can change during the extraction process (e.g., records updated or deleted).
  • Pagination Gaps: If the API paginates data dynamically, it may skip records or retrieve incomplete pages due to changes in the underlying dataset during runtime.

Solution

Setting the OData Parameter: paging=snapshot.
The paging=snapshot parameter ensures that the OData API captures a consistent snapshot of the dataset at the time of the query execution. This prevents records from being missed during the extraction process.

Step-by-step guide to implement

Step 1: Access the Dataflow

  • Log in to SAP Datasphere.
  • Navigate to the Data Builder and open the Dataflow where the issue occurs.

Step 2: Modify the OData Query Parameters

  • In the Source Node of the dataflow, locate the OData Parameter for the SuccessFactors data source.

  • Click on the Edit OData Parameters to open the OData Parameters dialogue and select the option “paging” and enter the value “snapshot”.     

Step 3: Save and Deploy the Dataflow

  • Save the changes to the dataflow.
  • Delete the data from the Target Table and Re-Run the dataflow and monitor the logs to confirm successful data extraction.
  • Compare the source data in SuccessFactors with the data in the SAP Datasphere table to ensure no records are missing.

 

Integrating Picklist Attributes of SAP SuccessFactors in Datasphere

 When integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Datasphere via the OData API, a common issue users face is that picklist attributes do not appear in the target table after creating a Data Flow. This can lead to incomplete or unreadable data in reports and dashboards, as fields like Job Title, Department, Business Unit, and Country might only display IDs instead of meaningful descriptions.

Root Cause

Picklist fields in SF are exposed as navigation attributes by SF OData API, which by default are not consumed by SF OData connection.

Workaround

  • For source tables from OData remote connections, you can edit the depth properties of a selected source table in the OData Properties section.

      

  • Click Edit OData Properties to open the OData Properties dialogue. You can set the depth of the source table to either 1 or 2.

The depth of an OData object refers to the level of related entities that are included in the response when querying the OData service. The depth is by default set to 1 so that only the properties of the requested entity are returned. You can change the depth to 2 to include a second level. Depth is useful when you want to optimize performance by controlling the amount of data returned in a single request.

Limitations

When the depth is set to 2, the Columns section shows the columns from the two levels and the following problems can occur:

  • The Data Preview cannot show more than one collection. The columns from the second collection cannot be previewed.
  • The Data Flow run will fail if the dataset is large. Retaining columns from just one level and deleting the rest fixes the issue. To improve performance, store picklist values separately and join them in Dataflows or as Dimension Associations.

Documentation links relevant to integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Datasphere:

SAP Help Portal

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2735876

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003136568

Acquiring, Preparing, and Modeling Data with SAP Datasphere | Help Guide

entry.title: Read Audit Report Configuration
entry.content:
I would like to share how to configure read audit configuration and how to classify HRIS fields into Sensitive Personal Data (SPD) fields. It is a lightweight tracking tool on the field level access, it helps authorised personnel's need not have to deep dive into the report of data changes, parallelly they can have an eagle eye on tracking whoever has field access and read the classified "Sensitive Personal Data" fields either of their user profile (or) other employment profile.                                             

                                                     Pre-requisites & Configurations

Pre-requisites (RBP - Permissions)

Admin Centre Permissions

  • View Read & Audit Configuration
  • Edit Read & Change Audit Configuration
  • Generate Read Audit Reports

Configurations:

Action 1 🤺  - In Search tool -> Type (Manage Audit Configurations)

Select "Read Audit" tab, followed by enable the "Read Access Logging" toggle button and save it.

 

Quota & Usage: Now you will be start able to see the quota & Usage section.

Key Understanding on Quota & Usage:  

📢 Understanding 1 - Consolidated up to 15 fields only, you can make use of those fields to get audited.

📢 Understanding 2 - Out of 15 fields, the quota for standard fields is only for “5” fields, and the quota for custom fields is only for “10” fields.

📢 Understanding 3 – You cannot make all the existing and enabled fields in the system get read audited; the fields which you see in the “Audit Configuration” only can get audited.

📢 Understanding 4 –

Standard Fields – These are predefined by SAP as Sensitive Personal Data (SPD) fields. 

💡 You can identify with help of predefine label .We don't have control on categorizing it is a Standard / Custom fields.

💡Custom Fields – Apart from pre-identified standard fields, the rest are all custom fields.

Fields from (ONB / Recruitment /HRIS Elements / Standard MDF Objects / Employee Profile)

Action 2 🤺🤺 - Choose  at bottom of the window, will lead ✈️ you to the configuration of different verticals of data fields exist in the system.

The split of field quotas assigned to each section, here 3/3 - standards fields fully utilized, custom field not utilized under this particular section.

Button "Go to Configuration Page" will lead 🚙 you to corresponding configuration page to enable the field as "Sensitive Personal Data"

Action 3 🤺🤺🤺 - How to switch on the field to get read audit.

Go to the respective fields, select "Details", 😎"Log read access"😎 is the button to switch on the field to get audited and save it.

💡- Indicator to know the field is now enabled for audit.

Action 4 🤺🤺🤺🤺 - For standard MDF you need to go to "Configure object definition" to enable the field for read audit.

 

👨‍🎓👩‍🎓- Now run the read audit report through, now you can be able track the info, who are all read the classified "SDP" fields of either their (or) other users. In compliance to quota of 15 fields, you can see the context key & value from "01" to "15". You can only see the field information not with values.

entry.title: Role-Based Permissions Quick Guide for Implementations
entry.content:
Key Considerations When Creating a New Role:

  1. Clearly define which processes will be managed by the role. This ensures that permissions align with the tasks and responsibilities associated with the role. Example for HR role the tool access becomes crucial to manage HR tasks example data update in manage data or position org chat or manage position. Whereas for an employee role these HR tools access is not required.
  2. Identify who will be in the granted population and who will be the target population. This helps in setting appropriate access levels and maintaining data security. Identify criteria to create the granted group example static groups will have user or username whereas dynamic group can be combination of a field from position or job along with Foundation object. Create target groups using foundation objects like employing entity or country etc.
  3. Determine the relationship between the granted and target populations. For example, is it a line manager and direct report, or HR and employee? Understanding these relationships is crucial for setting accurate permissions. The associations of role can be enabled using relationships like line manager, matrix manager, HR business partners, custom manager etc.
  4. Decide the level of security required based on the permissions. For instance, roles created for employees typically have more restrictions compared to roles created for Global Centers of Excellence (CoE), where organizational and related information may be less restricted. For HR roles as well organisation data access is granted to fill in the data while creating positions or add new employee processes.
  5. Enable reporting permissions as per the requirement. Example for some roles there might be a requirement to only enable reporting access while restricting system access.
    • Finalize the functionality which need to be enabled Adhoc, Canvas, or Story Reports.
    • Then enable relevant permissions like for adhoc domain grant separate domain access for create and run permissions under Reports permission section.
    • For canvas enable functional permission named canvas and detailed reporting under Analytics permission section.
    • For story create story permission in Reports permission section and then relevant data access from each module. Do not forget to enable user access in manage user section.
    • If the role is created for Performance, Compensation, or Variable Pay module then template access and document related permission need to be enabled. Also enable executive review.
    • Finally enable general permissions example: Report Center, schedule permissions, share reports to roles etc.

 

Basic Steps to Review Permissions in a Newly Created Role

  1. Before creating any new role check the existing roles to make sure there is really a need to create new.
  2. Check if any non-targeted permissions or admin permissions are enabled which when overlapped might enable more permissions than intended. Example OData admin, manage business configurations, etc should not be enabled for HR roles.
  3. Verify that the permissions are in line with the "need to know" principle. Enable sensitive data fields considering the information from the legal point of view to avoid any data incident.
  4. If any MDF objects permission is granted example employing entity, ensure that restrictions are added in role to group association to limit the values of employing entity for local HR configuration.
  5. Enable the inclusion or exclusion checkboxes in role association to exclude HR to have same permission for their own profile.
  6. Identify if the permissions of the new role are not overlapping with existing roles where the granted and target are the same as the new role. Example first name, last name fields if globally enabled for all users to see everyone’s first name and last name then these permissions are not required to be enabled in any other role as the global role will enable the access for all users.
  7. Ensure the naming convention limits the characters in a single association to be less than 1000 characters. If many groups need to be added in single association, then the character length of all groups together should not exceed 1000. This association will also get flagged in check tool.
  8. If possible, limit the number of associations to decrease performance issues. The performance issues when editing any role are proportional to number of associations. In other words, too many associations will cause performance issues when updating that particular role.
  9. Test the role for few negative scenarios maintain data security. Example scenarios:
    • HR should not be able to hire an employee outside target.
    • HR cannot report on employee outside target population.
    • Line manager should only see and report on direct repartees.
    • Homepage tiles are enabled as per requirement.

entry.title: Employee Central Head of Department Automatism (PART 2)
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

Looking back on this first blog of 2021, Employee Central Head of Unit Automatism, and moving forward with the idea of ​​always improving automation proposals, this blog part 2 proposes a more complete and efficient automation so that whenever we have a change in the head of department, the system does so autonomously but always based on the Position Head of Department, which is our key data field.

CONTEXT

It's common for customers to ask for a "Position Head of Department" in addition to the "Person Head of Department." Along with this request, there's often a desire for automation—when the Person Head changes, the update should automatically be reflected in the Department based on the already assigned Position Head. As per standard, the "Person Head of Department" field requires manual updates, however, and as stated above, a new proposal comes to make this automation possible in a simpler way.

Let's look at the details of how the new proposal turned out.

SOLUTION

Step 1

Create a custom field in the Position Object that will be used to trigger the Head of Department update.

                                             Image from demo

Step 2

Create a business rule that will update the new custom field when the "To be Hired" (TBH) field is updated, only for managerial positions and only if the position is assigned as Head of Department.

                                             Image from demo

Assign the rule onSave in the Position Object.

Step 3

Create an Intelligent Services Center (ISC) and an Integration Center (IC) to Update the Head of Department when the position triggers it, so you must have a "flag" in the Position to mark if it is a head/managerial or not.

                                             Image from demo

ISC Note: A rule was created to only trigger in case the employee’s position is assigned as the head of his/her department.

Integration Center Details:

Here I am using the source table as Position Object and the target table as Department Object.

Mapping:

  • Position start date to the Department start date
  • Position department code to the Department object code
  • Head of Department is a calculated field to either clean up or assign the Position Head incumbent.

                                             Image from demo

This concludes the configuration part. Now let's take a look on the expected results.

EXPECTED RESULTS

  1. Stephanie Thorn is the head of department, and she was terminated on 31/01/2025.

Before Termination:

          Image from demo

After Termination:

Job Info

                                             Image from demo

Position

                                             Image from demo

Department

                                             Image from demo

      2. Hired a new Manager to the Head of Department Position

Job Info

                                             Image from demo

Position

                                             Image from demo

Department

                                             Image from demo

NOTE: Please note that for the system to trigger Position ISC you must enable the "Raise events when a position is created or updated" setting under "Position Management Settings > Integration".

CONCLUSION

This blog post described how to automate the Person Head of Department based on the Position Head of Department using ISC and IC, covering cases where the incumbent of the Position Head changes due to terminations, transfers, new hires.

With the proposed solution we aim to prevent manual maintenance and increase data quality.

Looking forward to your comments and questions.

entry.title: Termination processes and when Offboarding can be triggered
entry.content:
When an employee is terminated, all the necessary information to terminate the contract will be sent to the payroll system, and any other stakeholders will be informed. Standard offboarding will be triggered immediately once the termination is approved in Employee Central. But what happens if the person who resigned is a talent? Typically, organizations try to retain talent, and if the negotiation is successful, HR needs to proceed with the manual cancellation of the termination event and offboarding. It's also necessary to check if the termination has already been replicated in the payroll system and to remove the termination of the contract. In this blog, written with @BarbaraSerio ,there is an alternative solution to use offboarding as a pre-termination process.

Step to configure

  • Create an event reason 'Pre-termination' as a data change event. This way, the employee status will remain active.

  • If an event derivation rule is configured in Employee Central, add the event reason to the rule based on customer requirements (for example, based on the value of a specific field in the Job Information portlet).

  • Configure the business rule for Offboarding adding this event reason 

  • Configure a specific program for the negotiation 

HR can start the process by adding the data change event and offboarding will be triggered. If the negotiation fails, then a termination can be added manually in Employee Central.
